sections.content: Visual distress signals are used to attract the attention of nearby vessels or aircraft when they are within visual range. SOLAS requires vessels to carry approved pyrotechnic distress signals. These are most effective at close range and should be used when rescuers are in sight or expected to be nearby.

rows: Close-range signaling to ships or aircraft in sight
Daytime signaling, marks position for aircraft
Daytime, dropped in water, marks position

sections.content: Pyrotechnics have expiry dates and must be replaced when expired. Store them in a cool, dry, accessible location. Always fire parachute rockets downwind and at a slight angle to avoid them blowing back onto the vessel.

options.text: When vessels or aircraft are in sight or expected nearby
Only when all other communication methods have failed

sections.explanation: Pyrotechnics are visual signals with limited range. They should be used when rescuers (vessels or aircraft) are in visual range or expected to arrive soon. Using them too early wastes a limited supply.
